text: Code refactoring is the process of improving code structure without changing its behavior. It's like cleaning and organizing a room: everything stays in the same place functionally, but it becomes easier to find and use things. When developers refactor, they focus on making the code more readable and easier to maintain. This reduces technical debt, which is the accumulated cost of taking shortcuts in code. By refactoring regularly, teams can avoid messy, confusing code that slows down future development. In short, refactoring helps keep software healthy, manageable, and efficient over time.
